Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated creatinine, significant proteinuria, and low urine specific gravity, suggesting impaired renal function, possibly chronic kidney disease. Urine findings (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal) further support renal pathology. Blood glucose is normal, but HbA1c is unexpectedly low—this may indicate a lab error or altered red cell turnover. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Recommend nephrology evaluation and repeat HbA1c for clarification.
